title: cmd: Fix help text. --config specifies a dir not a regular file
body:
This `--config` option was initially added here:
https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/4e4c3e3dbffd6c8fc2c1a8674a581aed61f2d629

Under the hood this simply modifies env to set DOCKER_CONFIG=<passed in string>

The DOCKER_CONFIG env var is used as a directory that contains multiple config files... of which podman and container libs probably only use `$DIR/config.json`.
See: https://docs.docker.com/reference/cli/docker/#environment-variables

The old CMD and help text was misleading... if we point the at a regular file we can see errors like:
```
$ touch /tmp/foo/tmpcr9zrx71
$ /bin/podman --config /tmp/foo/tmpcr9zrx71 build -t foobar:latest
Error: creating build container: initializing source docker://quay.io/centos/centos:stream9: getting username and password: reading JSON file "/tmp/foo/tmpcr9zrx71/config.json": open /tmp/foo/tmpcr9zrx71/config.json: not a directory
```
^^ In this case we had created `/tmp/foo/tmpcr9zrx71` as a regular file.

```release-note
Clarify --config <str> CMD option help text
```

comments.body:
Do you think we should at parse time just check that the path supplied in `--config` exists, and is a dir?
Or should we just yeet over into the DOCKER_CONFIG env var?
I'm not opposed to adding additional parsing code (e.g. to check that the path actually is a directory) if it gets us better error messages

Added simple checks for --config option. Must exist and be accessible, must be a directory
```
[~/prog/podman main] touch /tmp/some_file
[~/prog/podman main] mkdir /tmp/some_dir

[~/prog/podman main] go run ./cmd/podman --config /tmp/no_exist
Supplied --config folder (/tmp/no_exist) does exist or is not accessible: stat /tmp/no_exist: no such file or directoryexit status 1

[~/prog/podman main] go run ./cmd/podman --config /tmp/some_file
Supplied --config file (/tmp/some_file) is not a directoryexit status 1

[~/prog/podman main] go run ./cmd/podman --config /tmp/some_dir
Manage pods, containers and images

Usage:
  podman [options] [command]
```

Tests added, and they run as expected. I see this though in CI
```
[+0158s] Ran 1 of 2349 Specs in 22.343 seconds
[+0158s] SUCCESS! -- 1 Passed | 0 Failed | 0 Pending | 2348 Skipped
[+0158s] 
[+0158s] 
[+0158s] Ginkgo ran 1 suite in 2m17.818205841s
[+0158s] Test Suite Passed
[+0158s] Detected Programmatic Focus - setting exit status to 197
[+0158s] make[1]: *** [Makefile:673: ginkgo-run] Error 197
```

Perhaps `main` is just in a bad state? ^ That does not make sense to me: Test Suite Passed... FAIL :D

Also I checked the commit before mine and I dont think anything fails on my commit that doesnt also fail on HEAD~1
I was comparing against
```
commit 37dc5fdf89eca091f317e0c6145ff59fc0526d77 (HEAD -> main)
Merge: 8461dff1a 7e1ea7481
Author: openshift-merge-bot[bot] <148852131+openshift-merge-bot[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Date:   Mon Apr 7 18:03:11 2025 +0000

    Merge pull request #25820 from Luap99/maintainers

    Maintainers.md: fix two github nicks
```

I think your system test failures are real, though they don't seem to make sense; an EEXIST on a folder that Podman ENOENTs on?

Never seen that integration failure before. @Luap99 have you?

Changed validation code, current state:
- [allowed] pointing at a config path that does not exist at all
- [fails] pointing at some config path that is inaccessible (or os.Stat() fails AND NOT and exists fail)
- [fails] poting at some regular file with --config (only Dirs are allowed!)

cc @mheon this seem good to you?

Those rules SGTM.

CI is green

Hey @mheon is this separate from the approved tag? 

> Waiting for status to be reported — Not mergeable. Needs lgtm label.
Yeah, needs an LGTM from a second maintainer to merge

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
